insight: CSS animations using transform and opacity are compositor-only properties and do NOT cause Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS). Elements with opacity:0 still occupy layout space. Only actual document flow changes (width/height/top/left/margin changes, display:none toggling) affect CLS. IntersectionObserver-based scroll reveals with transform animations are CLS-safe.

When using inline style animations in React (e.g., style={{animation: '...'}}) instead of CSS classes, the prefers-reduced-motion media query from index.css won't apply because inline styles have higher specificity. Must check window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') in JavaScript and set animation to 'none' explicitly.

IntersectionObserver is not available in jsdom (vitest/testing-library). Hooks using IntersectionObserver must check typeof IntersectionObserver === 'undefined' and fall back gracefully (e.g., immediately reveal). Without this check, tests will throw ReferenceError: IntersectionObserver is not defined.
